channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: How Giiguy Gamambi Preschool turned a 5-day A&R notice into a lesson in everyday quality
item.description: Giiguy Gamambi Preschool, located on Gumbaynggirr Country, has shared its first experience of undergoing assessment and rating (A&R) under New South Wales' five-business-day notice period, offering practical insights for services preparing for their own assessment visits.
item.content:encoded: Giiguy Gamambi Preschool, located on Gumbaynggirr Country, has shared its first experience of undergoing assessment and rating (A&R) under New South Wales' five-business-day notice period, offering practical insights for services preparing for their own assessment visits.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: A child safe policy is only effective if the people it is designed to protect can understand it
item.description: Across the early childhood education and care (ECEC) sector, services are investing significant time in reviewing and strengthening their child safety policies. But even a technically compliant document may have limited value if it is generic, text-heavy or written in language that children, families and staff struggle to make sense of.
item.content:encoded: Across the early childhood education and care (ECEC) sector, services are investing significant time in reviewing and strengthening their child safety policies. But even a technically compliant document may have limited value if it is generic, text-heavy or written in language that children, families and staff struggle to make sense of.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Advanced child safety training to be released
item.description: Australia's early childhood education and care (ECEC) sector will enter the next stage of national child safety reform when advanced child safety training will become available from July 2026, expanding on the mandatory foundation modules introduced earlier this year.
item.content:encoded: Australia's early childhood education and care (ECEC) sector will enter the next stage of national child safety reform when advanced child safety training will become available from July 2026, expanding on the mandatory foundation modules introduced earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Child online safety is a collective effort, let’s start acting like it
item.description: The concept, “it takes a village to raise a child” has been used time and time again to highlight how an entire community, extended family, neighbours, friends and teachers, all have a role to play in the safe and healthy upbringing of children.
item.content:encoded: The concept, “it takes a village to raise a child” has been used time and time again to highlight how an entire community, extended family, neighbours, friends and teachers, all have a role to play in the safe and healthy upbringing of children.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: The Atlantis Group meets the emerging ECEC leadership shortage with thirty years of people investment and the results to prove it
item.description: A leading Western Australian family-owned early childhood provider points to award-winning leadership culture, structured talent pathways and long-term succession planning as the sector's most urgent strategic response.
item.content:encoded: A leading Western Australian family-owned early childhood provider points to award-winning leadership culture, structured talent pathways and long-term succession planning as the sector's most urgent strategic response.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Reframing the conversation: why the words we use about early childhood education matter
item.description: How Australia talks about early childhood education and care (ECEC) influences how the profession is understood, valued and supported.
item.content:encoded: How Australia talks about early childhood education and care (ECEC) influences how the profession is understood, valued and supported.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Everyone experiences loss and grief – but that doesn’t make it any easier to talk with kids about it
item.description: Parents and educators sometimes avoid having conversations about grief with children because they don’t know what to say. Here are some tips for how to approach it.
item.content:encoded: Parents and educators sometimes avoid having conversations about grief with children because they don’t know what to say. Here are some tips for how to approach it.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: The Breadwinner: Comedy doesn’t need flawless dads, but we do need competent ones
item.description: Comedic portrayals of incompetent dads can distort public understanding of fathers’ essential and real-life contributions to children’s development.
item.content:encoded: Comedic portrayals of incompetent dads can distort public understanding of fathers’ essential and real-life contributions to children’s development.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Childcare workers have been guaranteed a pay bump. What’s the fine print?
item.description: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.content:encoded: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Toy Story 5 pits traditional toys vs a tablet. In real life, families can combine the two
item.description: The latest instalment of the film franchise addresses a dilemma many parents face: what happens to playtime when children become obsessed with screens?
item.content:encoded: The latest instalment of the film franchise addresses a dilemma many parents face: what happens to playtime when children become obsessed with screens?
